main.description: Read how the calling app's own notifications are currently routed for a user, so the app can render its in-app settings UI. Dual-authenticated exactly like setRouting (app JWT in the Authorization header + a user-issued `userToken`). It is a procedure, not a query, so the user token travels in the request body rather than the URL. Requires an active grant from the user to the app.

userToken.description: A user-issued atproto service-auth JWT (com.atproto.server.getServiceAuth) with aud = the relay's DID and lxm = pub.atmo.notify.getRouting.

route.description: App-wide route: a '+'-joined set of channel tokens (a bare channel push|telegram|email, or 'channel:<id>' for one delivery instance), 'off', or 'default' to inherit the account default.

defaultRoute.description: The user's account-default route: a '+'-joined set of channel tokens (bare channel or 'channel:<id>') or 'off'.

categories.description: Categories this app has used for the user, with their current routing.

targets.description: The user's deliverable targets (one per push device / telegram chat / email / etc.), so the app can render a full route picker — including channels not in the current route. Use a target's `id` in a `channel:<id>` route token. Labels are privacy-safe: a user-chosen name, else a generic one (no raw email/handle unless the user named it that).

type.description: The channel this target belongs to (push|telegram|email|dm|webhook).

id.description: Opaque, stable instance id — use it in a 'channel:<id>' route token.

label.description: Privacy-safe display name for the target.

description.description: Human description last seen for this category, if any.

route.description: Route for this category: a '+'-joined set of channel tokens (bare channel or 'channel:<id>'), 'off', or 'app' to inherit the app-wide route.
